findings.description: The PDF uses a three-column layout for agenda items (time, topic, presenter) with the presenter names right-aligned on the same line as the topic. The HTML collapses this into a single-column format with presenter names in parentheses inline with the topic text.
findings.evidence: PDF: Time slots in left column, topics in center, presenter names right-aligned (e.g., '10:00-10:10  Review Agenda and Brief Introductions  (Neelufa Sarwar)' spanning the full width). HTML: All content flows as a single column with presenter names appended in parentheses after the topic title.

findings.description: The document structure uses only h1 for 'AGENDA' but no sub-headings for the agenda items. The original PDF presents agenda items with visual separation (horizontal rules between items) but does not use explicit heading hierarchy either, so the flat paragraph structure is acceptable. No heading text is wrong.
findings.evidence:
All agenda items are rendered as

elements, which is a reasonable structural choice for this type of document.

findings.description: The HTML contains nested <main> elements, with the header content (title, location, date) appearing in a <header> inside the outer <main>, and then a second <main> element containing the agenda items. This creates a structural duplication issue with two <main> tags, though the actual text content is not duplicated.
findings.evidence: The outer <main> from the body template wraps a <header> and then another <main> element for the agenda content.
